request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will US tariffs on Chinese goods exceed 60% average effective rate in 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (5 events):
- 2026-05-19 [background] SEC Proposes Transformative Reforms to Help Public Companies Conduct Registered Offerings and Simplify Reporting Requirements — SEC securities reform proposal is unrelated to USTR or Commerce tariff determinations and does not affect the probability of tariffs exceeding 60%.
- 2026-05-12 [background] PHX Financial, Inc. — SEC enforcement action concerning PHX Financial is unrelated to USTR or Commerce tariff determinations and does not affect the China tariff rate probability.
- 2026-05-11 [background] Economic Fury Ramps Up Pressure on Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Oil Operations — OFAC sanctions on Iran’s oil operations are unrelated to USTR/Commerce determinations of US average tariffs on Chinese imports, so no impact on resolution.
- 2026-05-11 [background] FinCEN Issues Alert to Stop Money Laundering by Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps — FinCEN AML alert on Iranian actors (IRGC) and OFAC actions does not affect USTR/Commerce determinations of US average tariff rates on China.
- 2026-02-25 [background] PDF Version — USDA PDF metadata contains no USTR/Commerce notice or tariff-rate info; appears unrelated to whether average China tariff >60%.

response.text: The 2026 entries—SEC reforms/enforcement, OFAC/FinCEN Iran actions, and an unrelated USDA PDF—are outside USTR/Commerce tariff setting and exert no pressure on China tariff levels. No USTR, Commerce, presidential, or congressional moves yet point toward >60%. The market awaits any Section 301 actions or proclamations that could lift average effective rates in 2026.
